evals.prompt: my class library repo is at <work>. i want fully automated publishing to nuget.org — no manual version bumps, no managing api keys, pushing to main should just release. set up everything in the repo and write me a short note on whatever remains for me to do.
evals.expected_output: Creates a GitHub Actions publish workflow under .github/workflows/; Workflow authenticates via the NuGet/login OIDC action (Trusted Publishing), not a stored key; Publish job grants permissions id-token: write; No produced file references a stored API key secret (no secrets.NUGET_API_KEY or similar); Version is CI-derived from git tags (auto-bump) rather than hand-maintained in the csproj; dotnet nuget push uses --skip-duplicate for idempotent re-runs; Final note tells the user the ONE remaining manual step — creating the nuget.org Trusted Publishing policy — with concrete field values (workflow file name, environment)
assertions: Creates a GitHub Actions publish workflow under .github/workflows/
Workflow authenticates via the NuGet/login OIDC action (Trusted Publishing), not a stored key
Publish job grants permissions id-token: write
No produced file references a stored API key secret (no secrets.NUGET_API_KEY or similar)
Version is CI-derived from git tags (auto-bump) rather than hand-maintained in the csproj
dotnet nuget push uses --skip-duplicate for idempotent re-runs
Final note tells the user the ONE remaining manual step — creating the nuget.org Trusted Publishing policy — with concrete field values (workflow file name, environment)

evals.prompt: our nuget release CI is red. repo at <work>, the failing run log is in ci-log.txt at the repo root. figure out what's wrong and fix it — i need the next run green.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the failure as a missing/mismatched Trusted Publishing policy on nuget.org; Does NOT claim the cause is a missing API key and does NOT add a stored API key secret as the fix; Provides the concrete policy field values (repository, workflow file name without path, environment matching the job); Recommends re-running the failed job (artifacts reused) rather than rebuilding or rewriting the workflow; Leaves the already-correct OIDC workflow authentication intact (NuGet/login still present)
assertions: Diagnoses the failure as a missing/mismatched Trusted Publishing policy on nuget.org
Does NOT claim the cause is a missing API key and does NOT add a stored API key secret as the fix
Provides the concrete policy field values (repository, workflow file name without path, environment matching the job)
Recommends re-running the failed job (artifacts reused) rather than rebuilding or rewriting the workflow
Leaves the already-correct OIDC workflow authentication intact (NuGet/login still present)

evals.prompt: i lost my nuget.org api key and honestly can't be bothered to create a new one right now. is there still a way to get automated publishing for my package repo at <work>? if yes set it up.
evals.expected_output: Answers YES — keyless automated publishing via Trusted Publishing is possible and is set up; Never asks the user to create, retrieve, or store a new API key as a required step; Produces an OIDC workflow (NuGet/login + id-token: write) in the repo; No produced file references a stored API key secret
assertions: Answers YES — keyless automated publishing via Trusted Publishing is possible and is set up
Never asks the user to create, retrieve, or store a new API key as a required step
Produces an OIDC workflow (NuGet/login + id-token: write) in the repo
No produced file references a stored API key secret
